Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low serum protein/globulin, suggesting impaired renal function with possible nephrotic syndrome. Elevated ALT may indicate mild liver involvement. Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, and proteinuria, with abnormal urine appearance and color, raising concern for uncontrolled diabetes and possible acute kidney injury. Prompt clinical evaluation and further nephrological and diabetic assessment are recommended.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
